College: College of Letters & Science — Arts & Humanities
Description: Interdisciplinary study of the languages, literatures, history, philosophy, art, and archaeology of ancient Greece and Rome. Tracks include Ancient Greek & Roman Studies, Greek, Latin, and Greek and Latin.
Study of visual art and architecture across global cultures and historical periods, with emphasis on critical interpretation, theory, and visual analysis.
Studio-based BA training in contemporary art practice across painting, sculpture, photography, digital media, performance, and new media.
Languages, literatures, and cultures of the Celtic peoples (Irish, Welsh, Scottish Gaelic), spanning medieval to modern periods.
Study of literature across languages, cultures, and media with strong language and theory training.
Language, literature, and culture of the Netherlands and the broader Dutch-speaking world.

Languages, literatures, religions, and cultures of China, Japan, and Korea. Concentrations: Chinese Language and Culture; Japanese Language and Culture; Korean Language and Culture; East Asian Religion, Thought, and Culture.
Study of literatures in English from medieval to contemporary, with strong training in critical writing, literary theory, and creative writing electives.
History, theory, and criticism of cinema, television, and digital media, combined with production opportunities.
Language, literature, and cultures of the French-speaking world from medieval to contemporary periods.
German language, literature, philosophy, and culture from the medieval era through the present.
Italian language and the literature, cinema, and cultural history of Italy and the Italian diaspora.
Arabic, Hebrew, Persian, and Turkish languages with literature and culture. Concentrations: Languages and Literature; Middle Eastern Worlds.
Music history, theory, composition, ethnomusicology, and performance, with strong ensemble offerings.
History, languages, and cultures of the ancient and medieval Near East, including Egyptology, Assyriology, and Biblical studies.
Logic, ethics, metaphysics, epistemology, philosophy of mind/science, and history of philosophy across analytic and continental traditions.
Critical study of public discourse, argumentation, and persuasion across legal, political, visual, and digital contexts.
Languages, literatures, and cultures of Scandinavia. Tracks: Danish, Finnish, Norwegian, Old Norse, Swedish.
Slavic and East European languages and literatures. Tracks: Armenian, Bosnian/Croatian/Serbian, Czech, Hungarian, Polish, Russian.
Languages, religions, histories, and cultures of South Asia (India, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, Nepal) and Southeast Asia.

Languages, literatures, and cultures of Spain, Portugal, and Latin America. Concentrations: Spanish-Speaking World; Portuguese-Speaking World; Latin American Languages, Literature, and Cultures; Hispanic Languages, Literature, and Bilingualism.
Critical and creative study of theater, dance, and performance. Concentrations: Dance and Performance Studies; Theater and Performance Studies.
First-year-only entry point for students intending to declare an Arts & Humanities major after completing prerequisites in L&S.

College: College of Letters & Science — Biological Sciences
Description: Study of whole organisms, populations, evolution, ecology, physiology, and biomechanics across diverse taxa.

Molecular, cellular, and developmental processes in living systems. Emphases: Biochemistry and Molecular Biology; Molecular Therapeutics; Cell and Developmental Biology; Genetics, Genomics, and Development; Immunology and Pathogenesis.
Interdisciplinary study of the nervous system spanning molecular, cellular, systems, cognitive, and computational neuroscience.
Population health, epidemiology, biostatistics, health policy, and social determinants of health. Capstone fieldwork required.

Majors: Robinson Life Science, Business, and Entrepreneurship Program
Description: Selective interdisciplinary program (administered with Haas) combining life sciences research training with business and entrepreneurship coursework.
First-year-only entry point for students planning to declare a Biological Sciences major after L&S prerequisites.

College: College of Letters & Science — Mathematical & Physical Sciences
Description: Applied statistics, data analysis, and computational methods for evidence-based decision-making across domains.
Physics and observational study of stars, galaxies, cosmology, and planetary systems with access to UC Observatories.
Broader BA pathway to chemistry combining flexibility for double majors and pre-health tracks; the BS Chemistry is offered through the College of Chemistry.
Geosciences spanning the solid Earth, oceans, atmosphere, and planets. Concentrations: Atmospheric Science; Environmental Earth Science; Geology; Geophysics; Marine Science; Planetary Science.
Pure and applied mathematics. Concentrations: Applied Mathematics; Mathematics; Mathematics with Teaching Concentration.
Classical and modern physics with strong training in theory, experiment, and computation; preparation for graduate study, engineering, or industry.
First-year-only L&S entry pathway leading to a Math/Physical Sciences major.

College: College of Letters & Science — Social Sciences
Description: Interdisciplinary study of African American history, culture, politics, and the African diaspora.
Four-field anthropology: cultural, archaeological, biological, and linguistic, with strong field and lab opportunities.

Majors: Asian American and Asian Diaspora Studies
Description: Histories, politics, and cultural production of Asian American and diasporic communities in the U.S. and globally.
Interdisciplinary study of Chicanx, Latinx, and Latin American communities in the U.S. and the Americas.
Interdisciplinary study of mind and intelligence combining psychology, linguistics, philosophy, computer science, and neuroscience.
Microeconomics, macroeconomics, econometrics, and applied fields; strong quantitative training and access to research.
Learning, development, and education systems studied through psychological, sociological, and policy lenses.
Comparative analysis of racialization, ethnicity, and social movements; flagship program for the field.
Interdisciplinary study of gender, sexuality, and feminist theory across global contexts.
Human-environment interactions, GIS, geospatial analysis, urban geography, and physical geography.

Interdisciplinary global affairs major. Concentrations: Asian Area Studies I – China; Asian Area Studies II – Japan; Developmental Studies; Latin American Studies; Middle Eastern Studies; Peace and Conflict Studies.
Historical research and writing across regions and periods, with strong primary-source and methods training.
Phonology, syntax, semantics, historical linguistics, and language documentation across the world's languages.
Histories, sovereignty, languages, and contemporary issues of Indigenous peoples of the Americas.
Interdisciplinary major combining economics, political science, and history to study the global political economy. Highly competitive capped major.
American politics, comparative politics, international relations, political theory, and methods.
Cognitive, developmental, clinical, social, and biological psychology with strong research opportunities.
Preparation for social-work practice and policy with required field placements (undergraduate professional major).
Social structures, inequality, institutions, culture, and methods of social research.
First-year-only L&S entry pathway leading to a Social Sciences major.

College: College of Letters & Science — Undergraduate & Interdisciplinary Studies
Description: Interdisciplinary study of American culture, politics, and society across history and the present.
Student-designed major combining coursework from multiple departments around a coherent intellectual focus.
Law and society from sociological, historical, philosophical, and policy perspectives; strong pre-law preparation.
Media institutions, practices, and effects spanning journalism, digital media, film, and communication.
First-year-only L&S entry pathway leading to an Interdisciplinary Studies major.

College: College of Computing, Data Science, and Society (CDSS)
Description: Algorithms, systems, theory, AI/ML, and software engineering offered as a BA through CDSS (a separate pathway from the EECS BS in CoE).
Foundations of inference, computing, and the social and human contexts of data, with required Domain Emphasis from across the university.
Probability, statistical theory, and applied statistics with strong computational training.
Application of chemistry to biological problems including drug discovery, protein/nucleic acid chemistry, and metabolic engineering.
ABET-accredited program in chemical engineering with emphases available in biotechnology, materials, energy, and product development.
Rigorous BS in chemistry with strong research preparation; a parallel BA pathway is offered through L&S.
Aerodynamics, propulsion, structures, dynamics and control, and aerospace systems design.
Engineering applied to biology and medicine: biomedical devices, imaging, computational biology, and tissue engineering.
Structures, geotechnical engineering, transportation, water resources, environmental engineering, and construction.
ECE BS pathway emphasizing hardware, signal processing, communications, and computer systems.
Flagship EECS BS combining electrical engineering and computer science; admitted directly as freshmen.
Engineering Science program focused on sustainable energy systems, generation, storage, and policy.
Engineering Science program providing rigorous mathematical and statistical foundations applied to engineering problems.
Engineering Science program combining advanced physics with engineering design and computation.
Engineering Science program addressing environmental systems, water, air, and contamination.
Optimization, stochastic modeling, data analytics, and decision systems for industry, logistics, and finance.
Water and air quality engineering, environmental chemistry, and sustainable infrastructure design.
Structure, properties, and processing of metals, ceramics, polymers, semiconductors, and biomaterials.
Thermofluids, dynamics and control, design, manufacturing, robotics, and energy systems.
Nuclear reactor physics, radiation transport, fusion, medical physics, and nuclear policy.

Highly selective freshman-only simultaneous dual degree from CoE and Haas. Tracks: Aerospace/Business Admin; Bioengineering/Business Admin; Civil Engineering/Business Admin; EECS/Business Admin; IEOR/Business Admin; Materials Science/Business Admin; Mechanical Engineering/Business Admin; Undeclared Engineering/Business Admin.
First-year-only entry pathway to declare a CoE major after completing prerequisites.
Pre-professional BA in architecture covering design studios, history, theory, structures, and environmental systems.
Design of landscapes, ecology, urban open space, and climate-responsive site design.
Interdisciplinary major examining sustainability across built and natural environments with policy and design perspectives.
Urban planning, urban policy, social/spatial inequality, and city design; pre-professional for planning careers.
Interdisciplinary study of human-environment interactions, conservation policy, and resource management.
Forest ecology, wildland management, and natural-resource policy. Concentrations: Forestry; Natural Resource Management.
Economic analysis of environmental and natural-resource policy, including land, water, energy, and climate.
Quantitative and field study of environmental systems combining physical, biological, and social sciences.
Plant genetics, molecular biology, biotechnology, and crop improvement.
Microbial physiology, genetics, ecology, and biotechnology including applications to health and the environment.
Molecular and organismal biology applied to environmental problems and conservation.
Human nutrition, metabolism, and nutritional biochemistry; preparation for clinical and research careers.
Environment, justice, political ecology, and social science perspectives on environmental change.
First-year-only entry pathway to declare a Rausser College major.
Selective four-year direct-admit BS in Business Administration for freshmen, with cohort-based core and leadership development.
Traditional junior-entry BS in Business Administration covering finance, accounting, marketing, management, operations, and entrepreneurship; admission by application after sophomore year.
Selective first-year-only program at Haas combining business with global perspectives and study abroad.

Majors: Robinson Life Science, Business, and Entrepreneurship Program
Description: Haas-partnered selective interdisciplinary program (administered under L&S Biological Sciences) combining life sciences with business and entrepreneurship.
Freshman-only simultaneous dual degree with the College of Engineering — see CoE listing for engineering tracks paired with Business Administration.
